div.p: To all sons of Holy Mother Church Richard de Morville constable of the King of Scotland and William his son and heir send greeting. Know us to have granted and by this our present charter to have made firm to God and to the church of St Mary at Melrose and to the monks serving God in that place, for our souls and of all our ancestors' and successors' souls, a place in Whitelee inside the limits of the forest for making a vaccary, that is, for a hundred and twenty-six cows, or a sheep-fold, whatever seems better and more useful to them and their house; to be held and possessed freely and quitely by them for ever without any disturbance from us and of all our men and of all men against whom we shall have been able to maintain them and warrant the same possession to them; and one building in which they may make a dwelling for the brethren and their shepherds and one building also in which they may put their hay. We have granted these three buildings in pure and everlasting alms to the same monks to be built in the forementioned place at Whitelee and to be possessed for ever. And it is to be known that they shall build no other dwellings in the forest, but their shepherds shall have wattled huts under which the livestock being guarded shall lie when the need arises. With these witnesses: Jocelin bishop of Glasgow; Herbert dean of Glasgow; Thomas, clerk; Godfrey de Ros; Ralph Mackerel; Richard son of Sewar; Alan son of Aelsi; Gilbert of Lancaster; Richard son of Gilbert; Richard son of Geoffrey; William the bishop of Glasgow's steward; Master Roger of Newport.
